EPCW-360.summary: Update external actions on ep-core workflows (Beginning of June)
EPCW-360.description:
### **Context**

Some GitHub Actions runs shows such messages:

> Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/github-script@v7. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 2nd, 2026. Please check if updated versions of these actions are available that support Node.js 24. To opt into Node.js 24 now, set the FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true environment variable on the runner or in your workflow file. Once Node.js 24 becomes the default, you can temporarily opt out by setting ACTIONS_ALLOW_USE_UNSECURE_NODE_VERSION=true. For more information see: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/</custom> 

---

AENW-872.summary: [Microsite UCS] Implement microstie pre-transcoding pipeline and background image processing
AENW-872.description:
Add pre-transcoding support to the microsite flow. When a user uploads a video in the microsite form, start transcoding immediately (before Save) by uploading the asset to raw_video_assets S3 bucket. The TranscodingProcessor saves variants to a new MicrositePreTranscoding record when MicrositeVideo doesn't exist yet. At save time, Create/Update use cases pick up ready variants instantly — eliminating wait time.  
Additionally, process background images via ImageMagick at upload time, producing 4K WebP and FHD JPG variants.  
Acceptance Criteria

* \[ \] POST /api/v3_beta/microsites/pre_transcode accepts upload_id, saves Asset, creates MicrositePreTranscoding(status: in_progress), enqueues PreTranscodeVideo job
* \[ \] PreTranscodeVideo job uploads asset to raw_video_assets S3 bucket (key: microsite/{microsite_id}/{asset_id}/{filename})
* \[ \] TranscodingProcessor saves variants to MicrositePreTranscoding when MicrositeVideo not found (deferred pickup)
* \[ \] Create/Update use cases accept asset_id in videos params, set MicrositeVideo status from MicrositePreTranscoding (ready/transcoding/pending)
* \[ \] Videos with asset_id skip re-upload to S3 (already in pipeline)
* \[ \] GenerateHtml enqueued immediately when all videos ready at save time
* \[ \] Background image processed to 4K WebP (quality 85) + FHD 1920x1080 JPG (quality 90)
* \[ \] Variants stored in microsites.background_variants jsonb column
* \[ \] GenerateHtml uses resolution-specific background URLs with backward-compat fallback
* \[ \] Microsite can be created without videos (background only)
* \[ \] Existing old-flow (upload_id without pre-transcoding) unchanged
* \[ \] Unit tests for all new paths

Technical Notes

* New table microsite_pre_transcodings (asset_id unique FK, status enum, variants jsonb)
* New column microsites.background_variants (jsonb, default {})
* Route: POST (/:role_type(/:role_id))/api/v3_beta/microsites/pre_transcode
* Resque job Microsites::Tasks::PreTranscodeVideo with retry (4 attempts, exponential backoff)
* Background processing via mini_magick gem + ImageMagick (already in project)
* S3 key format for video: microsite_video/{asset_id}/{filename} → triggers EventBridge → Lambda → UCS
* UCS Kafka callback matches back via asset_id (original_asset_id field)
* Depends on Lambda handler supporting microsite key format (separate task)

AETVP-622.summary: [rtb-trader] Polls & Surveys ad with 2-4 answer options possible
AETVP-622.description:
## Task Description

**As a** Samsung Ads Advertiser,   
**I want** to have flexible configuration options for the number of answer choices in Polls & Surveys creatives   
**so that** I can tailor my interactive advertising campaigns to match my specific engagement strategy and content requirements.

## Business Context

Currently, Polls & Surveys creatives have limited flexibility in configuring the number of answer options available to viewers. Advertisers need the ability to create more targeted and contextually appropriate interactive experiences by choosing between 2, 3, or 4 answer options depending on their campaign objectives and content strategy. This flexibility allows advertisers to optimize user engagement by presenting the most relevant number of choices for their specific polls or surveys, ultimately improving campaign effectiveness and viewer interaction rates. The enhanced configuration capability enables advertisers to create more personalized and engaging interactive video advertising experiences on TV Plus.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Current Polls & Surveys form under Interactive Video Ads form should be extended by option to choose number of answers displayed in the overlay
* Form adjusts number of inputs to reflect chosen number of the answers (2, 3, or 4 options)

PEA-4217.summary: [Audience Service][EMR][Incremental Reach] Spark version of the Incremental Reach reporty decomision
PEA-4217.description:
The Incremental Reach report is currently generated in two versions: UDW and Spark. The goal is to completely disable the Spark version of the Incremental Reach report in the production environment. This will allow for cost savings associated with report generation and will limit the scope of changes related to the EMR cluster upgrade.  

Acceptance Criteria:

1. The generation of the Incremental Reach report in the Spark version should be completely halted.
2. The process of replicating report generation requests from the UDW instance to Spark should be stopped.
3. Remove the EMR cluster responsible for the Incremental Reach report generation should be removed on the PRD and  STAGING environments.
4. Historical data in `ri_data` SQL database schema should be archived. Content of the `INCREMENTAL_REACH_SPARK_REPORT` table should be truncated - on PRD and STAGING environments.

MAW-437.summary: [SRE] Silver Layer Pipeline — Deployment, Flyway Migrations & Snowpark Orchestration
MAW-437.description:
‌

**As a DevOps engineer**, 

**I want to** establish a complete deployment pipeline for the Silver layer — including Flyway-managed Snowflake DDL migrations, Snowpark transformation deployment as stored procedures and scheduled tasks — integrated with our existing Helm/ArgoCD infrastructure, 

**so that** data flows automatically from Snowflake Bronze tables to the Silver ECAPI table, with schema evolution handled safely and the entire pipeline observable and deployable through our standard CI/CD process.

## Priority

**P0 — Critical** (Depends on Story 1; enables data to flow through the full medallion architecture)

## Context & Problem Statement

Story <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/MAW-436</custom>  delivers the **generation** of Silver DDL, Snowpark transformations, and JSON Schemas from the master context. However, none of these artifacts are deployed or operational. Currently:

* The Go server writes validated events to Bronze table
* There is no Snowflake Silver table created
* There is no Snowpark transformation running to convert Bronze → Silver
* There is no migration strategy for schema evolution (what happens when ECAPI adds a field?)
* There is no CI/CD pipeline for deploying Snowpark code or Snowflake DDL changes

The Silver layer must be a **production-ready pipeline** that:

1. Automatically creates/evolves the Silver ECAPI table in Snowflake via Flyway migrations
2. Runs Snowpark transformations as scheduled or streaming jobs
3. Handles schema evolution without data loss
4. Is fully integrated into our deployment pipeline (ArgoCD + Helm)

## Goal

Establish the **complete deployment pipeline for the Silver layer**, from DDL migration management (Flyway) through Snowpark job orchestration, integrated with our existing Kubernetes/ArgoCD deployment infrastructure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Flyway-based database migration management for the Silver ECAPI table in Snowflake implemented
- [ ]  Silver layer deployment into the existing Kubernetes/ArgoCD/Helm pipeline integrated

‌

AETVP-623.summary: [tvp-remotion] Polls & Surveys with two options below centered video + FALLBACK
AETVP-623.description:
## Task Description

**As a** Samsung Ads Advertiser,   
**I want** Remotion to properly generate Polls & Surveys ads with two answer options and fallback QR codes   
**so that** my interactive advertising campaigns display correctly on TV Plus and provide reliable viewer engagement even when overlay functionality is unavailable.

## Business Context

When advertisers create Polls & Surveys campaigns in the DSP, the creative content must be processed by Remotion to generate the video stream that appears during TV Plus ad breaks. For campaigns using the two-option variant of Polls & Surveys ads, Remotion needs to generate the proper video layout with static content that supports the interactive overlay elements. Additionally, a fallback mechanism using QR codes embedded directly in the stream video ensures that viewers can still participate in the poll or survey even if the overlay functionality fails or is unavailable. This ensures campaign continuity and maximizes advertiser ROI by maintaining engagement opportunities across different viewing scenarios and technical conditions.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Remotion generates two answer version of Polls & Surveys ad with proper layout implementation
* Fallback QR code is visible in output video for alternative engagement when overlay is unavailable
* Layout aligned with Figma design specifications for consistent visual presentation

AETVP-609.summary: [rtb-trader] Extract part of ad background for usage as overlay background
AETVP-609.description:
### Task Description

**As a** Samsung Ads Advertiser,   
**I want** the interactive overlay of my advertisement to have a seamless background that matches the underlying ad content   
**so that** there is no visual bleeding or transparency issues that detract from the user experience and engagement with my poll/survey.

### Business Context

Samsung Ads is building interactive Polls & Surveys for TV Plus that allow viewers to engage with advertisements using their remote control during ad breaks. This transforms traditional one-way TV advertising into an interactive engagement channel that provides valuable audience insights to advertisers while increasing viewer engagement and time-on-screen.

The current challenge is ensuring that the interactive overlay layer appears visually correct without any bleeding or transparency artifacts from the underlying advertisement content. To create the optimal user experience, the interactive layer needs to appear transparent while actually reusing the background it is covering, rather than being truly transparent (due to overlay failure fallback content being imprinted in the video).

This background extraction capability is essential for the Creative Canvas Interactive Video Ad framework that supports multiple interaction types including QR codes, click-to-email, product carousels, and the new polls & surveys functionality. The extracted background will be integrated into the rtb-trader component and made available through the Data Activation pipeline for use in the TV Plus interactive ad ecosystem.

### Acceptance Criteria

* Overlay background image is properly extracted from advertisement background image
* Overlay background image is saved as image asset of the creative
* URL to newly created asset is exposed for Data Activation pipeline consumption
* Background extraction works seamlessly with existing Creative Canvas Interactive Video Ad workflow
* No visual bleeding or transparency artifacts occur in the interactive overlay display
* Solution integrates with existing rtb-trader component architecture
* Solution extracts different parts of background depending on chosen number of the answers (for 2 answer version needs part of bottom of the background, for 3 or 4 answers right side of background needs to be extracted)

### What to Change

```
Database migration — add a column to store the ID of the extracted background asset (or use existing assets table with a new key) 
Creatives::InteractiveVideoAd model — add association for the new asset (pattern identical to logo_image_assoc / background_image_assoc) 
ProcessRemotionRequest task — after generating QR code (or instead of it for poll_survey) add step: open background image, cut 586×1080px from x=1334, save as new asset, attach to creative. Step should be called on every task run (i.e., on every background image change, because task is triggered by REMOTION_VIDEO_REGENRATION_NEEDED_FIELDS) 
GraphQL object type — [FOR DISCUSSION] expose URL of the new asset so frontend can read it (optional, needed only if frontend should display preview)
```

### When to trigger extraction

On first attachment of creative to flight   
On every background image change (because background_image_upload_id is in REMOTION_VIDEO_REGENRATION_NEEDED_FIELDS → triggers ProcessRemotionRequest)

‌

Worth noting: when applying background in overlay it will most likely be set with rule `background-position: right` to mitigate need for pixel perfect cutting & placing the background

AENW-1047.summary: [CP Improvements] Hide Right Align CTA Switch Behind Feature Flag
AENW-1047.description:
## Context

The `CTAAlignmentSwitch` component allows users to toggle CTA alignment (left/right) for Immersive and Masthead layouts. This feature should be hidden behind a localStorage flag so anyone can enable it manually via browser DevTools without needing env variable changes or redeployment.

## How to enable

In browser DevTools console:

```js
localStorage.setItem("feature_right_align", "true");
```

Then refresh the page. To disable:

```js
localStorage.removeItem("feature_right_align");
```

## Scope

Gate the `CTAAlignmentSwitch` rendering behind `localStorage.getItem("feature_right_align") === "true"` in both places where it's used:

1. **Main layout** — `layout-main.tsx`
2. **Share view** — `ShareContentView/index.tsx`

## Default Behavior

When the flag is off (switch hidden), the CTA is **always left-aligned** (default). The right-align option is only available when the switch is visible. Even when flag is on CTA is still left-aligned by default

## Acceptance Criteria

* \[ \] When `feature_right_align` is not in localStorage or is not `"true"`, the CTA alignment switch is hidden and CTA defaults to left-aligned
* \[ \] When `localStorage.setItem("feature_right_align", "true")` + refresh, the switch appears as before (Immersive + Masthead layouts)
* \[ \] Share view also respects the flag
* \[ \] No TypeScript errors
* \[ \] No env variable or deployment config changes needed

Update terraform-aws-global-network-subnets to split endpoint creation from DNS creation:

  create_jfrog_endpoint     = true   # Creates VPC endpoint + SG only  
  create_jfrog_endpoint_dns = true   # Creates PHZ + alias records (requires endpoint)

Currently both are created together with create_jfrog_endpoint = true. The module must be updated so that:

* create_jfrog_endpoint = true creates only the VPC endpoint and security group
* create_jfrog_endpoint_dns = true creates the Route53 PHZ and alias A records (depends on endpoint existing)

This allows a safe two-phase migration: deploy all endpoints first, then flip DNS per-VPC.

* Some VPCs are not create using global network module (e.g. UDW) but they are in scope.

---

**Requirements**

* Release new module version
* Inventory all VPCs (update Confluence page with full list)
* Enable create_jfrog_endpoint = true on all Dev/Test VPCs
* Enable create_jfrog_endpoint = true on all Shared VPCs
* Enable create_jfrog_endpoint = true on all Production VPCs
* Collect all vpce-id outputs
* Register all endpoints in MyJFrog portal
* Confirm all endpoints status = available

---

**Acceptance Criteria**

* Module supports independent endpoint and DNS creation
* All target VPCs have JFrog VPC endpoint created

* No traffic is routed yet — existing NAT path still active
* VPC inventory documented on Confluence page

RSW-1427.summary: Cumulative Reach: Respect exclude_dsp_reach flag in CR calculation
RSW-1427.description:
### Summary

The `exclude_dsp_reach` flag (set per report in `optimal_reach_input` parameters) is respected by the Optimal Reach audience-building procedure (`R__build_or_audiences.sql`) but is **not** respected by the Cumulative Reach calculation (`R__calculate_cumulative_reach.sql`). This causes DSP/PMP reach to be incorrectly included in the Cumulative Reach totals when the flag is set to `TRUE`.

---

### Context

In Audience Insights, the `exclude_dsp_reach` flag controls whether DSP and PMP platform input tables are included in the Target Audience for Post-Campaign Reporting. When enabled, OR correctly excludes these platforms — but the Cumulative Reach ML-side computation does not check this flag, leading to inconsistent data between OR and CR sections of the same report.

This is a **blocker for Cumulative Reach market expansion** (Canada and beyond), as any market where this flag is in use will produce incorrect CR numbers.

---

### Current Behavior

* `R__build_or_audiences.sql` reads `report_params:parameters:optimal_reach_input:exclude_dsp_reach` and excludes DSP/PMP tables when `TRUE`
* `R__calculate_cumulative_reach.sql` does **not** read or respect this flag — DSP/PMP reach is always included in CR calculation regardless of the flag value

---

### Expected Behavior

* `R__calculate_cumulative_reach.sql` should read the `exclude_dsp_reach` flag from `ai_report_status.report_params`
* When `TRUE`, DSP and PMP platform input tables should be excluded from the Cumulative Reach computation (same logic as in `R__build_or_audiences.sql`)

---

### Implementation Reference

**Pattern to follow** (from `R__build_or_audiences.sql`):

```
-- Read the flag
SELECT NVL(report_params:parameters:optimal_reach_input:exclude_dsp_reach::BOOLEAN, FALSE)
INTO :v_is_exclude_dsp_reach
FROM ai_report_status
WHERE report_id = :p_report_id
    AND report_version = :p_report_version
    AND report_processing_status = 'IN_PROGRESS'
ORDER BY report_invocation_version DESC
LIMIT 1;

-- Apply exclusion
WHERE ...
    AND NOT :v_is_exclude_dsp_reach
```

**Repo:** `adgear/audience-insights-snowflake` **File to modify:** `apps/audience-insights/migrations/sp/R__calculate_cumulative_reach.sql` **Reference file:** `apps/audience-insights/migrations/sp/R__build_or_audiences.sql`

---

### Acceptance Criteria

* Given a report with `exclude_dsp_reach = TRUE`
* When the Cumulative Reach calculation runs
* Then DSP and PMP platform reach is excluded from CR totals (consistent with OR behavior)  
* Given a report with `exclude_dsp_reach = FALSE` (or not set)
* When the Cumulative Reach calculation runs
* Then DSP and PMP platform reach is included (backward-compatible, no change in default behavior)

---

### Validation

* Re-run an existing report where `exclude_dsp_reach = TRUE` and verify CR numbers no longer include DSP/PMP reach
* Compare OR Target Audience composition vs CR input — should be consistent

AENW-1032.summary: [CP Improvements] Creative Preview 2026 Unified First Screen — Design Fixes
AENW-1032.description:
## Summary

Configure alerting rules for microsite frontend health metrics. Alerts should notify the team when error rates spike, page loads are consistently slow, or video playback is degraded — enabling proactive response before campaigns are impacted.

## Context

* Metrics flow: TV → metrics.js → telemetry endpoint → Kafka → metrics store → Grafana/Alertmanager
* Alerts should fire to Slack (non-critical) and PagerDuty (critical, if SLA-impacting)
* Microsites serve ads on Samsung TVs — a broken microsite = lost revenue + bad advertiser experience
* Reference dashboard: "Microsite Frontend Health" (separate task)

## Acceptance Criteria

### Critical Alerts (Slack + PagerDuty)

* \[ \] **High error rate**: JS error rate > 10% of page loads over 15min window
* \[ \] **Video playback failure**: Video error rate > 5% over 15min window
* \[ \] **Complete outage**: Zero metrics received for > 10min (when expected traffic > 0)

## Context

The `Immersive Masthead 2026` preview in Creative Preview tool has several visual discrepancies compared to the updated design spec.

AENW-907.summary: Spike: Investigate ad serving pipeline for Samsung TV+
AENW-907.description:
**Description:**

Investigate and document the ad serving pipeline for Samsung TV+ platform. The current ad response generator supports Samsung TV Native, which requests ads from the bidder. TV+ is a different platform that uses a different mechanism for requesting ads — likely involving delivery directly rather than the bidder.

 **Goal:**

Build a clear understanding of the TV+ ad serving flow and determine what changes are needed in ad response generator to support it.

**Areas to investigate:**

* Systems involved — identify all services/components in the TV+ ad serving chain (delivery, bidder, CDN, any intermediaries)
* Request format — what does the ad request to the server look like (endpoint, parameters, headers)
* Data dependencies — what data needs to exist for a TV+ ad to be served (flights, creatives, targeting, schedules)
* Response format — what is returned (VAST XML, JSON, other) and how it differs from TV Native responses
* Impact on ad response generator — identify which modules/scripts need changes and what new components are required

**Expected output:**

  A document covering:

1. End-to-end request flow for TV+ ad serving
2. List of systems/services involved with their roles
3. **Example request/response (or pointer to where to find one)**
4. Proposed changes to ad response generator — what to add and where

‌

  **Acceptance Criteria:**

* \[ \] TV+ ad serving flow is documented end-to-end (from request trigger to final response)
* \[ \] All involved systems/services are identified with their roles
* \[ \] Request format is documented (endpoint, parameters, headers, example)
* \[ \] Response format is documented with example
* \[ \] Key differences from TV Native pipeline are listed
* \[ \] A list of required changes in ad response generator is provided, specifying:

    * Which existing files/modules need modification
    * What new modules/scripts need to be created
    * What new parameters/arguments are needed (e.g. new CLI flags)
    * What new data dependencies or external services are required
    
* \[ \] Rough effort estimate for implementation is provided
* Jira task to do implementation in ad response generator

RSW-1306.summary: [AU] E2E Segment Generation Validation — Pipeline Trigger & Size Consistency on PROD
RSW-1306.description:
### 1. Summary

Validate that the Optimal Reach segment generation pipeline triggers correctly on PROD for Australia and that segment sizes reported in Audience Builder / Pre-Campaign Insights / Post-Campaign Reporting are consistent with the ML job predictions.

This is a pipeline smoke test, not a full UAT. The goal is to confirm the end-to-end flow works in production before running comprehensive user acceptance testing.

### 2. Context

* AU infrastructure is in place:
* feature flags enabled <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-833</custom> 
* ACL migration deployed
* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-832</custom> 
* Audience Builder ads section enabled <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-834</custom> 
* Data verification completed <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-3">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-860</custom> 
* AU uses HDMI Streaming Stick-based Ads Dataset + In-App ACR data for AVOD modeling

### 3. Scope

For 3-5 AU advertisers, trigger OR segment generation on PROD and compare the segment size output across:

| Check Point | Source | What to Compare |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ML Job output | ML pipeline | Predicted segment size from ML job |
| Audience Builder (AB) | DSP UI — Audience Builder | Segment size shown in AB after segment lands |
| Pre-Campaign Insights (AI) | DSP UI — OR Insights report | Segment size shown in pre-campaign report |
| Post-Campaign Reporting | DSP UI — Post-Campaign report | Segment size shown in post-campaign report (if applicable) |

**Test configurations** (follow Canada/ESBO pattern):

1. Linear Advertiser/Brand Only
2. Linear + Streaming Advertiser/Brand Only
3. Linear + Streaming mix

### 4. Steps

#### Step 1: Audience Builder, Segment Generation & Validation

1. Trigger OR segment generation via the ML pipeline (Airflow) for selected AU advertisers
2. Confirm the ML job completes successfully (no Airflow DAG failures)
3. Record the ML job predicted segment size
4. Navigate to Audience Builder confirm the generated segment appears for AU / Buyer 11
5. Record the segment size shown in AB
6. Compare AB segment size vs ML job output document discrepancy (≤5% tolerance = pass)

#### Step 2: Pre-Campaign Insights, Report Generation & Validation

1. Generate a Pre-Campaign Insights report for the same AU advertisers/brands
2. Confirm report generates successfully (no errors)
3. Record the segment size shown in the OR Insights report
4. Compare Pre-Campaign Insights segment size vs ML job output document discrepancy (≤5% tolerance = pass)

#### Step 3: Post-Campaign Reporting,  Report Pull & Output Check

1. Pull a Post-Campaign report for an AU advertiser with campaign delivery data (if available)
2. Confirm report generates successfully (no errors)
3. Verify incremental reach metrics and segment sizes are populated
4. Check outputs for data completeness and correctness

#### Step 4: Cross-Validation, AB vs Pre-Campaign Insights Size Comparison

1. For the same input data (same advertiser, same brand, same time window), compare segment sizes between Audience Builder and Pre-Campaign Insights
2. Document any discrepancies between AB and AI sizes
3. If difference >5%, investigate root cause (e.g., AD filtering logic, country filtering, exposure window differences)

Test records Confluence page: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-4">https://adgear.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/AGILE/pages/22925705410/AU+E2E+Segment+Generation+Validation+Pipeline+Trigger+Size+Consistency+on+PROD</custom> 

### 5. Acceptance Criteria

#### Step 1: Audience Builder

- [ ] ML pipeline triggers successfully for AU on PROD (no failures)
- [ ] Generated segments land in Audience Builder for AU / Buyer 11
- [ ] Segment size shown in AB matches ML job output within ≤5% tolerance
- [ ] If discrepancy >5%, root cause is documented

#### Step 2: Pre-Campaign Insights

- [ ] Pre-Campaign Insights report generates successfully for AU advertisers (no errors)
- [ ] Segment size shown in Pre-Campaign Insights matches ML job output within ≤5% tolerance
- [ ] If discrepancy >5%, root cause is documented

#### Step 3: Post-Campaign Reporting

- [ ] Post-Campaign report generates successfully for AU (no errors)
- [ ] Incremental reach metrics and segment sizes are populated and non-zero
- [ ] Report outputs are complete (no missing fields or broken calculations)

#### Step 4: Cross-Validation (AB vs Pre-Campaign Insights)

- [ ] For same input data, AB and Pre-Campaign Insights segment sizes compared
- [ ] Discrepancy between AB and AI is within ≤5% tolerance
- [ ] If discrepancy >5%, root cause is documented (known filtering differences are acceptable if explained)

### 6. Out of Scope

* Full UAT testing (separate ticket, later phase)
* Performance/latency benchmarking
* Multi-buyer validation (only Buyer 11 for now)
* Beta client campaign execution

---

### Key Observations from Canada

From the Canada test, typical results were:

* **Green (pass):** ≤2.5% difference between AI and AB
* **Yellow (pass with note):** 5-7% difference — usually explained by AD country filtering logic (AB filters by report country, AI filters by exposure country)
* **Expected zero cases:** Some advertisers had 0 segments in both AI and AB due to missing exposure data for the target country — valid but should be documented

RSW-1039.summary: [BE] Add GET endpoint for fetching presets from AI-backend
RSW-1039.description:
### Summary

Expose a REST endpoint that returns available app group presets filtered by country. The frontend's "Presets" dropdown in the Select Applications modal will call this endpoint to populate the list of selectable presets.

### Context

* ai_reporting_service — fasthttp/router REST API
* Presets are country-specific (DR-5): the endpoint must filter by country code
* Each preset returned must include: preset ID, display name, list of apps with their IDs and weighting values
* The frontend needs enough data in a single call to auto-populate app selection and weighting when a preset is chosen (DR-3)

### Flow

1. Frontend calls `GET /presets?country={countryCode}` (or similar path)
2. Handler validates country parameter
3. Service layer queries the presets table for active presets matching the country
4. Response returns an ordered list of presets, each containing: ID, display name, and apps array (app ID + weight)

### Scope

* New handler in `pkg/handlers/` — register route via existing pattern (`RegisterXxxRoutes()`)
* Repository interface + Postgres implementation in `pkg/domain/repository/`
* Service layer in `pkg/services/` (or co-located with existing related service)
* Request validation: country code is required, must be a supported country
* Auth: existing JWT + HMAC middleware (same as other endpoints)
* Response contract: JSON array of presets with nested apps

### API Contract (proposed)

```
GET /presets?country=DE

Response 200:
{
  "presets": [
    {
      "id": "uuid",
      "name": "German Streaming Competitors",
      "country": "DE",
      "apps": [
        {
          "appId": "abc-123",
          "weight": 100
        },
        {
          "appId": "def-456",
          "weight": 20
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Out of Scope

* CRUD endpoints for preset management (admin UI is future phase)
* Feature flag gating (presets are static config, not a toggleable feature)
* Caching (can be added later if latency is a concern)

### Dependencies

* <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/RSW-1038</custom>

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Endpoint registered and accessible with valid JWT auth
- [ ] Returns presets filtered by country code, ordered by display order
- [ ] Returns empty array (not error) for countries with no presets
- [ ] Returns 400 for missing/invalid country parameter
- [ ] Repository interface with `//go:generate` mockgen directive
- [ ] Unit tests for handler (mocked service) and service (mocked repository)
- [ ] `make test` passes, coverage ≥ 80%

ASPW-637.summary: consumerd ready file logic is broken
ASPW-637.description:
_As a_ platform engineer, _I want_ to investigate why the `deployed_at` file is created before all required files are downloaded and ready to serve, _so that_ dependent services (e.g. RTB Bidder) do not start with incomplete data.

## Business Context

Consumer Daemon can create the `deployed_at` marker file even though some files failed to download. Dependent services use this marker as a readiness signal and begin their startup process — which then fails because required files are missing. This applies to both sidecar and DaemonSet deployment modes.

The issue was observed in RTB Bidder, where the bidder started and failed due to missing GeoIP files, entering a retry loop until ConsumerD's next scheduled poll cycle downloaded the missing file. This causes unnecessary startup delays and error noise, and may affect any consumer relying on the `deployed_at` signal.

GeoIP files are not checked, because they are not listed in an explict way. Only their folder is listed. The contents of this folder is not checked. Additional checks need to be added for the contents of such folders.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Root cause identified: determine why the freshness/existence checks in `WriteMetaDataGenerationFile` do not prevent `deployed_at` creation when a download has failed
* Document which specific condition is not covered (missing file, stale file, or file in a config section not checked)
* Propose a fix that ensures `deployed_at` is only written when ALL configured files and folders are present and fresh
* Verify with unit tests that the fix does not introduce false negatives

EPCW-304.summary: Eliminate workload restart requirement during RKE1 recovery
EPCW-304.description:
### Context

During RKE1 recovery (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/EPCW-95</custom>), user workloads require restart.

**Root Cause:** Worker nodes need to be re-registered to the cluster during recovery, which causes workloads to restart.

The procedure for RKE1 has following sequence:

1. Master nodes recover and become Active
2. Worker nodes 'disappear' from Rancher
3. Worker nodes are re-registered via SSH command (manual operation)
4. During re-registration, workloads are restarted

### Goal

Investigate if we can eliminate or minimize workload restarts during worker node re-registration.

### Requirements

* We MUST analyze why workload restart is necessary during worker node re-registration
* We MUST determine if there's a way to keep workloads running during the process
* We MUST evaluate if worker nodes can rejoin automatically instead of manual re-registration
* We MUST document findings and any workarounds in <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/20903526687/Kubernetes+cluster+recovery+after+all+master+loss#RKE1</custom>  
* We MUST implement the solution, if solvable

## Problems

### 1. Stale IAM role references

Some sub-stacks (e.g. `ssp-test/variables.tf`) still reference IAM roles that no longer exist. Provider configuration should be unified to use named AWS profiles, as already done in `adgearprod/provider.tf`.

### 2. Missing `.terraform.lock.hcl`

The provider lock file is absent. Without it, provider versions are non-deterministic across runs and contributors.

### 3. Unapplied stacks

Several sub-stacks have pending changes that have never been applied, leaving the real infrastructure out of sync with the Terraform state.

### 4. Dead VPC references

Some stacks reference VPCs that no longer exist in AWS. These need to be identified and removed to prevent apply errors and confusion.

### 5. `count` instead of `for_each` — non-idempotent changes

Resources are managed with `count` rather than `for_each`. This means any reordering or removal causes index shifts, leading to unintended resource replacements instead of targeted changes.

## Requirements

* We MUST fix all issues 1-5 on all stack under: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://github.com/adgear/terraform/tree/master/aws/adgearprod/nacl-block</custom> which belong to EP.

MAW-435.description: **As an** AdOps specialist, **I want** CAPI conversion events to automatically create segments (like Web Pixel), **So that** I can use these segments in Conversion Groups without custom configuration --- ## 1. Codebase Analysis — Key Findings ### 1.1 Single Table Inheritance (STI) — All Segments in ONE Table All segment types are stored in a **single** `segments` table using Rails STI. The `type` column differentiates them. **Current STI types** (defined in `Segments::Base.sti_types_map`): | Type | Model Class | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `standard` | `Segments::Standard` | Web Pixel segments — deploys segment tag, has `external_id`, associated `web_action` | | `universal` | `Segments::Universal` | Universal segments — has `targeting_builder` (boolean expression), `piggyback_html` | | `offline` | `Segments::Offline` | Offline conversion segments — requires `offline_conversion_provider_id` | **DB constraint** enforces allowed types: ```sql -- segments_type_check: type IN ('standard', 'universal', 'offline') ``` **Another DB constraint** enforces offline-specific rules: ```sql -- validate_offline: (type = 'offline' AND offline_conversion_provider_id IS NOT NULL) ``` ### 1.2 `segments` Table Schema | Column | Type | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | | `id` | serial | PK | | `type` | string(255) | STI type: `standard`, `universal`, `offline` | | `buyer_id` | integer | FK to buyers, NOT NULL | | `name` | string(255) | | | `description` | text | | | `ttl` | integer | Default: 1296000 (15 days), range: 1h–60d | | `state` | string(255) | State machine: `active`, `archived` | | `adgear_type` | string(255) | `conversion` or `data_profile` | | `external_id` | string(255) | External identifier | | `environment` | string(255) | `production`, `cossette_production`, `adgrx_production` | | `meta` | hstore | Default: `{}`, flexible key-value metadata | | `piggyback_html` | text | Universal segments only | | `offline_conversion_provider_id` | bigint | Offline segments only | | `created_at` / `updated_at` | datetime | | **Unique index**: `[buyer_id, environment, adgear_type, external_id]` ### 1.3 GraphQL Mutations — Current Pattern Each segment type has its own create/update mutation pair: | Mutation | Model | Service | | --- | --- | --- | | `create_standard_segment` | `Segments::Standard` | `Services::Segment::Save` | | `create_universal_segment` | `Segments::Universal` | `Services::Segment::Universal` | | `create_offline_segment` | `Segments::Offline` | `Services::Segment::Save` | **Input types per segment type:** * **Standard**: `name`, `description`, `ttl`, `associated_web_action` * **Universal**: `name`, `description`, `ttl`, `piggyback_html`, `targeting_builder`, `targeting_builder_array`, `associated_web_action` * **Offline**: `name`, `description`, `offline_conversion_provider_id` **GraphQL Enum** `SegmentType`: `offline`, `standard`, `universal` ### 1.4 Conversion Group Integration — Critical Validation `ConversionGroup` has many `segments` through `conversion_group_segment_memberships`. **Key validation** in `ConversionGroupSegmentMembership#segment_type_valid`: ```ruby valid_types = case conversion_group.strategyable when OfflineAttributionStrategy [:offline] when SegmentAttributionStrategy [:standard, :universal] else [] end ``` This means: * **Offline Conversion Groups** → only `offline` segments * **Segment Attribution Conversion Groups** → only `standard` or `universal` segments * Any new segment type must be explicitly added here to be usable in Conversion Groups ### 1.5 Web Pixel → Segment Relationship The "Web Pixel" pipeline in the DSP context works as follows: 1. **Segment creation**: A `Segments::Standard` record is created via `create_standard_segment` mutation 2. **Segment tag deployment**: `Segments::Standard` auto-deploys a JS tag (`after_commit :deploy_segment_tag!`) to WebDAV 3. **Web Action association**: Segments can be associated with a `WebAction` via `segment_web_actions` join table 4. **Audience builder**: `AudienceAcrSegments::WebsitePixel` links audiences to segments (via `website_pixel_id` FK) 5. **Boolean expression**: `BuildBooleanExpression` builds `WEBSITE_PIXEL` events referencing `segment_ids` ### 1.6 Custom STI Types Mechanism The project uses `Concerns::CustomStiTypes` which overrides Rails default STI to use a custom type name → class mapping. To add a new type: 1. Add entry to `sti_types_map` in `Segments::Base` 2. Create new model class inheriting from `Segments::Base` 3. Update DB check constraint to include the new type 4. Update GraphQL enum `SegmentType` --- ## 2. Recommended Approach: New STI Type `capi` ### 2.1 Why a New Type (vs. Reusing `standard`)? | Aspect | New `capi` type | Reuse `standard` + tag | | --- | --- | --- | | Source identification | Built-in (type = `capi`) | Requires `meta` or `adgear_type` hack | | Filtering in UI/API | Simple `WHERE type = 'capi'` | Complex join or meta query | | Conversion Group validation | Explicit, type-safe | Must check meta/tag | | Different creation logic | Clean separation (no web action, no tag deploy) | Must skip web action logic conditionally | | Follows existing pattern | ✅ Same as `offline` was added | ❌ Breaks pattern | | DB-level enforcement | ✅ Check constraint | ❌ Application-level only | **Recommendation**: Add a new `capi` STI type, following the same pattern used for `offline` segments. This is the cleanest approach because: * CAPI segments have fundamentally different creation logic (server-side events, no pixel tag to deploy) * The type itself serves as the "CAPI" source label (satisfies "Could have" AC) * Clean integration with Conversion Groups * Follows established pattern in the codebase ### 2.2 What CAPI Segments Do NOT Need Compared to `Standard` segments: * ❌ No `deploy_segment_tag!` (CAPI events come server-side, no JS tag) * ❌ No `associated_web_action` (no pixel fire event) * ❌ No `external_id` uniqueness validation (CAPI uses `pixel_id` concept) Compared to `Offline` segments: * ❌ No `offline_conversion_provider_id` (CAPI is not offline conversion) --- ## 3. Detailed Requirements ### 3.1 Database Changes #### 3.1.1 Migration: Add `capi` to segments type check constraint ```ruby class AddCapiSegmentType < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0] def up # Update check constraint to include 'capi' execute <<-SQL ALTER TABLE segments DROP CONSTRAINT segments_type_check; ALTER TABLE segments ADD CONSTRAINT segments_type_check CHECK (type::text = ANY (ARRAY['standard'::character varying::text, 'universal'::character varying::text, 'offline'::character varying::text, 'capi'::character varying::text])); SQL end def down execute <<-SQL ALTER TABLE segments DROP CONSTRAINT segments_type_check; ALTER TABLE segments ADD CONSTRAINT segments_type_check CHECK (type::text = ANY (ARRAY['standard'::character varying::text, 'universal'::character varying::text, 'offline'::character varying::text])); SQL end end ``` #### 3.1.2 Consideration: `pixel_id` storage CAPI segments need to store a `pixel_id` to group events from one CAPI endpoint. Options: * **Option A**: Use existing `external_id` column (recommended — already indexed, follows `Standard` pattern) * **Option B**: Use `meta` hstore column (e.g., `meta['pixel_id']`) * **Option C**: Add a dedicated `pixel_id` column **Recommendation**: Option A — use `external_id` to store the CAPI `pixel_id`. This reuses existing infrastructure and the unique index `[buyer_id, environment, adgear_type, external_id]` ensures one segment per pixel_id per buyer. ### 3.2 Model Changes #### 3.2.1 New model: `Segments::Capi` ```ruby # app/models/segments/capi.rb module Segments class Capi < Segments::Base include Concerns::TrackableChanges model_name.class_eval do def route_key 'segments' end def param_key 'segment' end end # CAPI segments use external_id as pixel_id # One segment per pixel_id (CAPI endpoint) validates :external_id, presence: true, uniqueness: { scope: [:buyer_id] } track_changes! do attribute :name attribute :external_id attribute :ttl end # No deploy_segment_tag! — CAPI events come server-side # No associated_web_action — no pixel fire event def self.model_arguments(role, _user = nil, _action = :index, _args = {}) remove_nil_args({ buyer_id: role.buyer_id }) end def self.accessible?(ctx, action, context = {}, model = nil) model ||= ::Segments::Capi super end end end ``` #### 3.2.2 Update `Segments::Base.sti_types_map` ```ruby def self.sti_types_map { offline: ::Segments::Offline, standard: ::Segments::Standard, universal: ::Segments::Universal, capi: ::Segments::Capi # NEW } end ``` #### 3.2.3 Add `capi?` helper to `Segments::Base` ```ruby def capi? type.to_sym == :capi end ``` #### 3.2.4 Update `Segments::Base.tag` method ```ruby def tag case type.to_sym when :standard "" when :universal buyer.universal_segment_tag when :capi # CAPI segments don't have a pixel tag — return empty or a descriptive string '' else '' end end ``` ### 3.3 GraphQL Changes #### 3.3.1 Update `Enums::SegmentType` ```ruby # app/graphql/enums/segment_type.rb %w( offline standard universal capi # NEW ).each do |type| value type, I18n.t("graphql.enums.segment_type.#{type}", default: type) end ``` #### 3.3.2 New GraphQL Input type ```ruby # app/graphql/mutations/segment/capi/save_input.rb module Mutations module Segment module Capi class SaveInput < ::Mutations::BaseInput graphql_name 'CapiSegmentInput' description I18n.t('graphql.mutation.input_description', object_name: 'CapiSegment') argument :description, String, I18n.t('graphql.segments.description') argument :name, String, I18n.t('graphql.common.attributes.name') argument :ttl, Int, I18n.t('graphql.segments.ttl') argument :pixel_id, String, I18n.t('graphql.segments.pixel_id') # maps to external_id end end end end ``` #### 3.3.3 New GraphQL Error type ```ruby # app/graphql/mutations/segment/capi/save_errors.rb module Mutations module Segment module Capi class SaveErrors < ::Mutations::BaseSaveErrors graphql_name 'SaveCapiSegmentErrors' # Fields matching SaveInput end end end end ``` #### 3.3.4 New GraphQL Response type ```ruby # app/graphql/mutations/segment/capi/save_response.rb module Mutations module Segment module Capi SaveResponse = GraphQL::ObjectType.define do name 'SaveCapiSegmentResponse' field :success, !types.Boolean, I18n.t('graphql.mutation.success'), resolve: ->(result, _args, _ctx) { result.data.errors.blank? } field :data, Objects::Segment, I18n.t('graphql.mutation.save_data'), resolve: ->(result, _args, _ctx) { result.data.errors.any? ? nil : result.data } field :errors, Mutations::Segment::Capi::SaveErrors, I18n.t('graphql.mutation.errors'), resolve: ->(result, _args, _ctx) { result.data.errors.any? ? result.data : nil } end end end end ``` #### 3.3.5 New Mutations in Root ```ruby # app/graphql/mutations/root.rb — add alongside existing segment mutations field :create_capi_segment, Mutations::Segment::Capi::SaveResponse.to_non_null_type do description 'Create a CAPI Segment' guard ->(_, _, ctx) { Segments::Capi.accessible?(ctx, :create) } argument :input, Mutations::Segment::Capi::SaveInput.to_non_null_type resolve ->(_, args, ctx) { initial_attributes = Segments::Capi.model_arguments(ctx[:current_user].active_role) segment = Segments::Capi.new(initial_attributes) params = args.input.to_h.symbolize_keys # Map pixel_id to external_id params[:external_id] = params.delete(:pixel_id) if params.key?(:pixel_id) Services::Segment::Save.call(ctx[:current_ability], segment, params, ctx[:current_user]) } end field :update_capi_segment, Mutations::Segment::Capi::SaveResponse.to_non_null_type do description 'Update a CAPI Segment' guard ->(_, _, ctx) { Segments::Capi.accessible?(ctx, :update) } argument :input, Mutations::Segment::Capi::SaveInput.to_non_null_type argument :id, !types.Int, ID_ATTRIBUTE_DESCRIPTION resolve ->(_, args, ctx) { segment = Segments::Capi.records_accessible_by(ctx).find(args.id) params = args.input.to_h.symbolize_keys params[:external_id] = params.delete(:pixel_id) if params.key?(:pixel_id) Services::Segment::Save.call(ctx[:current_ability], segment, params, ctx[:current_user]) } end ``` ### 3.4 Conversion Group Integration #### 3.4.1 Update `ConversionGroupSegmentMembership#segment_type_valid` This is **critical** — without this change, CAPI segments cannot be added to Conversion Groups: ```ruby # app/models/conversion_group_segment_membership.rb def segment_type_valid valid_types = case conversion_group.strategyable when OfflineAttributionStrategy [:offline] when SegmentAttributionStrategy [:standard, :universal, :capi] # ADD :capi else [] end errors.add(:segment, :invalid_type) unless valid_types.include?(segment.type.to_sym) end ``` #### 3.4.2 Consideration: New Attribution Strategy? If CAPI segments need a **different attribution logic** than standard/web pixel segments, a new `CapiAttributionStrategy` class may be needed. However, for the POC, reusing `SegmentAttributionStrategy` (which covers `standard` and `universal`) is sufficient. **Question for product**: Should CAPI segments use the same attribution strategy as Web Pixel segments (`SegmentAttributionStrategy`), or do they need a dedicated strategy? ### 3.5 Segment Web Action Validation Update `Segments::Base` has a validation: ```ruby validates :offline_conversion_provider, presence: true, if: :offline? validates :offline_conversion_provider, absence: true, unless: :offline? ``` CAPI segments don't need `offline_conversion_provider`, so the existing `unless: :offline?` validation needs updating: ```ruby validates :offline_conversion_provider, absence: true, unless: -> { offline? } # This already works because capi? is not offline?, so the validation will require absence # for capi segments — which is correct. ``` ### 3.6 I18n Locales Add translations for the new `capi` segment type: ```yaml # config/locales/graphql.en.yml en: graphql: enums: segment_type: capi: "CAPI Segment" segments: pixel_id: "The CAPI pixel ID used to identify the conversion endpoint" ``` --- ## 4. Scope Summary — What Needs to Change ### Must Have (POC) | # | Change | File(s) | Type | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | 1 | Add `capi` to DB check constraint | New migration | DB | | 2 | Create `Segments::Capi` model | `app/models/segments/capi.rb` | Model | | 3 | Update `sti_types_map` | `app/models/segments/base.rb` | Model | | 4 | Add `capi?` helper | `app/models/segments/base.rb` | Model | | 5 | Update `tag` method for `:capi` | `app/models/segments/base.rb` | Model | | 6 | Add `capi` to GraphQL `SegmentType` enum | `app/graphql/enums/segment_type.rb` | GraphQL | | 7 | Create CAPI SaveInput | `app/graphql/mutations/segment/capi/save_input.rb` | GraphQL | | 8 | Create CAPI SaveErrors | `app/graphql/mutations/segment/capi/save_errors.rb` | GraphQL | | 9 | Create CAPI SaveResponse | `app/graphql/mutations/segment/capi/save_response.rb` | GraphQL | | 10 | Add `create_capi_segment` / `update_capi_segment` mutations | `app/graphql/mutations/root.rb` | GraphQL | | 11 | Update `segment_type_valid` to allow `:capi` | `app/models/conversion_group_segment_membership.rb` | Model | | 12 | Add I18n translations | `config/locales/graphql.en.yml` | Config | ### Could Have (POC) | # | Change | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 13 | CAPI source label/tag in UI | The `type = 'capi'` already provides this at data level; UI filtering by type is needed | | 14 | Auto-create segment on CAPI event | Backend event handler that auto-creates `Segments::Capi` when first CAPI event arrives for a `pixel_id` | | 15 | Dedicated `CapiAttributionStrategy` | If CAPI needs different attribution logic than Web Pixel | --- ## 5. Open Questions for Product 1. **TTL default**: What should the default TTL be for CAPI segments? (Standard: 15 days / 1296000 seconds) 2. **UI changes**: Does the DSP UI need updates to display/filter CAPI segments separately? --- ## 6. Architecture Diagram ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ segments table (STI) │ │ type: standard | universal | offline | capi (NEW) │ │ external_id: pixel_id (for capi) │ │ buyer_id, name, ttl, state, meta, ... │ └──────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ (polymorphic via conversion_group_segment_memberships) ▼ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ ConversionGroup │ │ strategyable: SegmentAttributionStrategy │ │ → valid segment types: standard, universal, capi │ │ strategyable: OfflineAttributionStrategy │ │ → valid segment types: offline │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ CAPI Event Flow (proposed): CAPI Event (pixel_id + conversion data) → Find or create Segments::Capi (by buyer_id + external_id=pixel_id) → Add user to segment storage → Segment available for Conversion Group ``` --- **Acceptance Criteria:** * CAPI events flow into existing segment storage (same backend as Web Pixel) * One segment created per pixel_id (all events from one CAPI endpoint grouped into single segment) * Segments available for Conversion Group creation **Could have (POC):** Segments tagged/labeled as "CAPI" source (vs "Pixel" source) for filtering **Product assumption:** Reuse existing Web Pixel → Segment pipeline. No new segment creation logic needed.

AETVP-611.description:
### Task Description

**As a** Samsung Ads Advertiser,   
**I want** the interactive overlay to use the extracted background image seamlessly   
**so that** any content imprinted into my advertisement background will not bleed through onto the polls & surveys interactive layer while maintaining the illusion of transparency in the right panel where interactive buttons are placed.

### Business Context

Samsung Ads is building interactive Polls & Surveys for TV Plus that transform traditional one-way TV advertising into an interactive engagement channel. As part of the Creative Canvas Interactive Video Ad framework, advertisers need assurance that their interactive overlays will display correctly without visual artifacts that could detract from the user experience and engagement.

The challenge is maintaining the visual illusion of transparency in the interactive overlay while preventing any background content from bleeding through. The rtb-trader component now extracts overlay background images from advertisement content (via <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/AETVP-609#icft=AETVP-609</custom>), and the data-activation pipeline includes these background image URLs in the ad response data (via <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/AETVP-610#icft=AETVP-610</custom>). The final step is implementing the actual usage of these extracted backgrounds in the tvp-interactive-ad overlay system.

This enhancement ensures that when viewers interact with polls and surveys during ad breaks using their remote control, the interactive overlay appears seamlessly integrated with the underlying advertisement. The right panel containing interactive buttons and poll content will maintain the visual appearance of transparency while actually utilizing the extracted background image to prevent any bleeding of underlying advertisement elements.

This capability is essential for advertiser confidence in the interactive ad format, as it guarantees that their creative content will display as intended without visual disruption from the interactive layer.

### Acceptance Criteria

* Interactive overlay utilizes the extracted background image provided in overlay_data
* No visual bleeding of underlying advertisement content through the interactive layer
* Right panel maintains illusion of transparency while using extracted background
* Solution works seamlessly with existing polls & surveys interactive functionality
* Background image integration does not impact overlay performance or load times
* Fallback behavior implemented for cases where background image is not available

### What to change

1. **Controller.ts** — in`getMessageHandlers()` add handler for `overlay_data`, which checkes whether `value.background_image_url` exists and if so, sets it as `background-image` on element `.survey-sidebar`
2. **pollsAndSurveys.types.ts** — add `background_image_url?: string` into type `SurveyOverlayData`
3. **Tests** — add test case for verification that `background_image_url` from `overlay_data` is being applied as CSS background for `.survey-sidebar`
